title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · Contract Lens: Compatible Rollout Order
summary: Tests mock amqplib method calls but never assert routing or failure behavior; deployment starts consumers before topology migration; a reconnect storm follows broker restart; rollback cannot read new message schemas. Planned artifact: Add and verify the compatible rollout order increment in tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.
Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Compatible Rollout Order · Theory Compatible Rollout Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5294 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Compatible Rollout Order contract for the supplied data.
